name: Tooltip Card
summary: A tooltip component that follows cursor position with smart viewport boundary detection and smooth animations.
detailedDescription: Tooltip Card implements a cursor-following tooltip that displays content near the mouse pointer while intelligently avoiding viewport boundaries. The component tracks mouse position relative to its container and calculates optimal tooltip placement using viewport dimensions. When the tooltip would extend beyond the right edge, it flips to appear left of the cursor; if it would go off the left edge, it positions at the container's left boundary. Similar logic applies vertically - the tooltip flips above the cursor if it would extend below the viewport, or above if it would go off the top. The tooltip uses Framer Motion's AnimatePresence for smooth enter/exit animations, with height calculated from scrollHeight for accurate sizing. Content is constrained to a minimum width of 15rem (240px) and positioned absolutely with 12px offset from cursor. The component handles both mouse and touch interactions, resetting positions on mouse leave. This creates a polished tooltip experience ideal for contextual help, preview cards, or inline documentation that needs to remain visible without obscuring content.
purpose: Display contextual information near cursor position
Prevent tooltip overflow with smart boundary detection
behavior: Tracks mouse position relative to container
Calculates optimal position based on viewport boundaries
Flips tooltip direction when approaching edges
Animates height from 0 to content scrollHeight
Handles both mouse and touch interactions
recommendations: Pairs well with testimonials, feature grids, and product highlight sections.
